title: Rent Payment Assistance * Medicaid Recipients - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Rent Payment Assistance * Medicaid Recipients 18 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV Disaster/Emergency Issues Domestic Violence Issues Domestic Violence Issues * African American Community Domestic Violence Issues * LGBTQ2+ Individuals Families with Children Hispanic/Latino Community Homeless Veterans Homelessness Issues LGBTQ2+ Individuals Medicaid Recipients Mental Health Issues Older Adults Pregnant Individuals Sexual Assault Issues Slavic Community Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Veterans Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ADVANCED HEALTH HEALTH RELATED SOCIAL NEEDS HOUSING SUPPORTS RENTAL ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Health-related social needs (HRSN) are basic needs that affect a person's health. These benefits help members who qualify with basic needs, including rent payment assistance and utility payment assistance, tenant support, and storage fees. Members can only receive HRSN housing services once per lifetime. Eligibility: Advanced Health members who meet all of the following: -Age 65 or older, children age 0-5, pregnant or gave birth in the past 12 months, OR anyone with a qualifying health condition -Have a household income 30% or less of the average yearly income where the applicant lives -Need support staying in housing -Have a lease or written rental agreement -Lack resources to prevent homelessness Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 269-7400 Visit Website More Details ADVANCED HEALTH FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (For individuals being discharged from the hospital; experiencing an extreme weather event; has a set move-in date for a new home and needs a temporary place to stay before then; or actively fleeing a domestic violence situation.) -Electronics such as cellphones, computers, and other communication devices -Specific educational materials and/or classes -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships and exercise classes/equipment -Health and substance use prevention support All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. These benefits help members who qualify with basic needs, including rent payment assistance and utility payment assistance, tenant support, and storage fees. Members can only receive HRSN housing services once per lifetime. Eligibility: Eastern Oregon CCO members who meet all of the following: -Age 65 or older, children age 0-5, pregnant or gave birth in the past 12 months, OR anyone with a qualifying health condition -Have a household income 30% or less of the average yearly income where the applicant lives -Need support staying in housing -Have a lease or written rental agreement -Lack resources to prevent homelessness Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (for individuals being discharged from the hospital or who have a set move-in date for a new home and need a temporary place to stay before then) -Electronics such as cell phones, computers, and other communication devices when there are no state programs available in the area -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships or home equipment -Beds and mattresses for children under 21 or for adults with a diagnosis of a back or spine condition -Car seats if there are no other county or programs available in the area All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Visit website to review additional eligibility criteria regarding specific programs and benefits. Members can only receive HRSN housing services once per lifetime. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 382-5920 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRILLIUM COMMUNITY HEALTH PLAN TRILLIUM COMMUNITY HEALTH PLAN SPRINGFIELD HEALTH RELATED SOCIAL NEEDS HOUSING SUPPORTS RENTAL ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Health-related social needs (HRSN) are basic needs that affect a person's health. These benefits help members who qualify with basic needs, including rent payment assistance and utility payment assistance, tenant support, and storage fees. Eligibility: Trillium members who meet all of the following: -Age 65 or older, children age 0-5, pregnant or gave birth in the past 12 months, OR anyone with a qualifying health condition -Have a household income 30% or less of the average yearly income where the applicant lives -Need support staying in housing -Have a lease or written rental agreement -Lack resources to prevent homelessness -Members can only receive HRSN housing services once per lifetime Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRILLIUM COMMUNITY HEALTH PLAN TRILLIUM COMMUNITY HEALTH PLAN SPRINGFIELD FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (For individuals being discharged from the hospital; experiencing an extreme weather event; has a set move-in date for a new home and needs a temporary place to stay before then; or actively fleeing a domestic violence situation.) -Electronics such as cellphones, computers, and other communication devices -Specific educational materials and/or classes -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships and exercise classes/equipment -Health and substance use prevention support All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify.
